label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. So the question is asking if following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: 1. Always face forward. 2. Take 2 steps backward. 3. Take 5 steps backward. 4. Take 5 steps right. 5. Take 9 steps right. Hmm, first, I need to visualize my movement. Let's assume "forward" is the initial direction I'm facing. When I take steps backward, that's opposite to forward. Then moving right would be perpendicular to my forward direction. Let me define a coordinate system. Let's say my starting point is (0, 0). Let's assume "forward" is in the positive y-direction. So, when I take steps backward, I'm moving in the negative y-direction. Moving right would be in the positive x-direction. Now let's track my position step by step: 1. Start at (0, 0). 2. Take 2 steps backward: I move 2 units in the negative y-direction. New position: (0, -2). 3. Take 5 steps backward: I move 5 more units in the negative y-direction. New position: (0, -7). 4. Take 5 steps right: I move 5 units in the positive x-direction. New position: (5, -7). 5. Take 9 steps right: I move 9 more units in the positive x-direction. New position: (14, -7). So my final position is (14, -7), which is not the same as my starting point (0, 0). I have moved 14 units to the right and 7 units down from where I started. Therefore, I don't return to the starting point.
